name: 何晶

work_experience.position: Engineer; Director of Defence Materials Organisation (from 1983)
Deputy Director of Engineering to President and CEO (1997)

person_relationships.description: Married Lee Hsien Loong in 1985; he was then a brigadier-general and rising politician
Succeeded Ho Ching as CEO of Temasek Holdings in October 2021
Appointed Ho Ching as CEO of Temasek Holdings in January 2004 when Goh was PM
Brother-in-law; Lee Hsien Yang's wife Lee Suet Fern was investigated in relation to Ho Ching's father-in-law Lee Kuan Yew's will

family_members.name: Lee Hsien Loong
family_members.industry_or_organization: Senior Minister of Singapore; former Prime Minister (2004-2024)

family_members.name: Li Xiuqi
family_members.industry_or_organization: Stepdaughter from Lee Hsien Loong's first marriage

family_members.name: Li Yipeng
family_members.industry_or_organization: Stepson from Lee Hsien Loong's first marriage

family_members.name: Li Hongyi
family_members.industry_or_organization: Deputy Director at Government Technology Agency of Singapore

political_stances.stance_content: Transformed Temasek into a global long-term investor with portfolio growing to over S$300 billion. Emphasised sustainable investing and transparency.
As Temasek Trust chairperson, focused on philanthropic initiatives and community development through structured giving.

major_achievements.achievement: Became President and CEO of Singapore Technologies
Named one of Time's 100 most influential people and Forbes' 3rd most powerful woman in business outside the US
As CEO for 17 years, oversaw transformation of Temasek into a global investor with portfolio growing to over S$380 billion
Awarded Sultan of Brunei Golden Jubilee Medal
Conferred First Class of The Most Exalted Order of Sultan Ibrahim Johor (SMIJ)
Ranked 33rd on Forbes' list of 100 most powerful women in the world

biography_summary: Ho Ching is a Singaporean businesswoman who served as CEO of Temasek Holdings from 2004 to 2021 and has been Chairperson of Temasek Trust since 2022. She is the second wife of former PM Lee Hsien Loong. An electrical engineer by training (NUS First Class Honours, Stanford MSc), she started at the Ministry of Defence in 1976, became Director of Defence Materials Organisation, then joined Singapore Technologies rising to President and CEO in 1997. She joined Temasek as Executive Director in 2002 and became CEO in 2004. Under her 17-year leadership, Temasek's portfolio grew to over S$380 billion. Named by Time as one of the 100 most influential people (2007) and ranked 33rd on Forbes' most powerful women list.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with person_relationships (Dilhan Pillay, Goh Chok Tong, Lee Hsien Yang), political stances, updated major achievements with proper dates, added stepchildren from Wikipedia and web search
